thesis: Swedish semiconductor pure-play specializing in mmWave beamforming chips for LEO satellite terminals and 5G infrastructure. Revenue growing 12%+ YoY with expanding gross margins and narrowing losses. Pre-profitability with ~2.5–3 years cash runway. Potential U.S. dual listing is a near-term catalyst. Asymmetric exposure to the satellite connectivity boom at a reasonable valuation for a growth semiconductor story.

drivers: LEO satellite terminal demand accelerates with major constellation deployments
5G mmWave design wins in Asia and North America
U.S. dual listing expands investor base and re-rates stock toward U.S. multiples
Photonics division recovers on data center optical interconnect demand for AI clusters

risks: Major customer concentration — loss of key satellite terminal program
Revenue stalls due to order delays or competitive displacement
5G mmWave market fails to materialize as expected
U.S. dual listing delayed or cancelled, removing key catalyst

base_case.assumptions: Steady satellite terminal growth; moderate 5G traction; revenue reaches SEK 450M by 2027; company achieves EBITDA breakeven by 2028

catalysts.event: Q2 2026 earnings — satellite order rebound after Q1 softness
Potential U.S. dual listing update / timeline
LEO constellation deployment milestones (Amazon Kuiper, Telesat)

key_risks: Customer concentration in satellite terminal market
Competition from larger RF semiconductor companies
